description:
Consumer report from trip-planner project.

Problem: There is no automated way to detect when helix.d.ts type definitions drift from the actual CEM (Custom Elements Manifest). This has led to 3+ critical bugs found in the trip-planner integration where TypeScript types suggested wrong attribute names.

Proposed tool: validate_type_definitions
- Reads the CEM JSON
- Reads the helix.d.ts file
- Compares every component's attributes, properties, events, and slots
- Reports mismatches: missing, extra, wrong types
- Returns structured diff that can be used by agents to auto-fix

Additionally: Add a CI gate that runs this validation on every PR to the helix library, preventing drift at the source.

Acceptance criteria:
- MCP tool exists and returns structured comparison
- Catches all known drift issues (tabs, skeleton, spinner)
- Can be run by Helixir CLI as well as MCP
- CI integration guide documented
title: feat: add validate_type_definitions MCP tool to catch CEM-to-types drift
